text.content:
Chapter 6 — Crown of Fangs. Serithtial in hand, the PCs return to a city under the queen's heel — and end it. Begin well into L15; the Sunken Queen expects L16+; the campaign closes at L17. The Final Harrowing (suit of crowns) opens the chapter.

The Korvosan Rebellion (Victory Points, mirroring Ch.4's Respect): the rebels (Cressida, Vencarlo, the Pharasmins, surviving allies — Amin, Grau, Ishani, Trinia, freed Maidens…) need Rebellion Points to hold the city while the PCs strike: expose Trifaccia +1 · kill Zarmangarof +2 · recruit Sabina +3 · each castle commander removed +1 · the castle taken +5. At 10+ RP the rebellion holds Korvosa through the endgame; under 5, the streets burn behind the party (feed the Epidemic Clock its catastrophe tier).

Threads resolving: every promise made since Chapter 1 comes home — Sabina's regret, Vencarlo's blade and mantle, Cinnabar's redemption (Kayltanya hunts her), Laori's long walk, Zellara's rest, the Arkonas' papers, the Epidemic Clock's final reading.

'Greetings, heroes of yesterday! My name is Trifaccia. Have you heard of me? No matter. I'm the true hero of the people of Korvosa, for I defend the loyal citizens of our beloved queen instead of fomenting chaos in the streets. As you can see, with the exception of a few clots of filthy rebels, Korvosa is at peace now, and soon prosperity and happiness will smile over it again.

I dare say that harmony first began to return not long after you vanished from our streets, and judging by the Yzahnum is a potent foe, but he'd rather face off against one PC in a duel than take them all on at once. His overall goal is to be able to either defeat that PC, or force the other PCs to step in to save their ally, therefore exposing their inherent lack of honor before a gathering crowd of gawkers. He hopes to make the PCs seem like part of the problem facing Korvosa, rather than a solution.

His great weakness is, of course, his true nature; if the PCs can expose the efreeti for what he really is (perhaps via antimagic field , or using enchantment magic to make him reveal his true form), his work as Trifaccia comes crumbling down.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yzahnumEfreeti01]{Yzahnum / Trifaccia (15)}
EVENT 1 — Korvosa's New HeroTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yzahnumEfreeti01]{Yzahnum} as 'Trifaccia' + 12 staged 'rebels' (L3 chaff): three hostile wishes from hiding, then the duel. Unmask the efreeti before the crowd and the persona — and the propaganda — dies (+1 RP).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sabinaMerrin0001]{Sabina Merrin (14)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Lvdykf6wfqzZBlZd]{Zarmangarof (16)}
EVENT 2 — The Gray MistressModerate · 90 XP @ L15

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sabinaMerrin0001]{Sabina Merrin} on Zarmangarof (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Lvdykf6wfqzZBlZd]{Black Dragon, Ancient} — run at CR-15 weight). She is sabotaging her own mount; when the geas-maddened dragon turns on her, she dismounts and fights it beside the PCs — then surrenders, confesses, and offers everything. The campaign's great redemption: take it.

pages.name: Part 2: The Assault on Castle Korvosa

text.content:
Castle Korvosa — the pyramid-borne palace, ~95 keyed areas (A1–A95) over five levels. Getting inside: Sabina's intelligence (post-Event 2), Venster's ghost, the Epochal Tower clockworks, the Seawatch approach (past @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras}), or the front door. Patrols: 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Palace Guards} per (Trivial · 0 XP @ L15 — chaff; 8 at gates), reinforcing in 1d4 rounds. Alarm escalates to commanders.

The truth the castle hides: Ileosa is gone — decamped to the Sunken Queen in the Mushfens with the Crown and the harvest, leaving simulacra, devils, and the garrison to bleed the rebellion. The castle assault's prize is the path: Togomor's notes, Venster's testimony, and the Hidden Way Below (A5).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A1-A25 — p.348.

  • The dungeon: the emptied Vault of Fangs (A1 — where the Crown was born) and the royal crypts (A3 — Eodred's dust).
  • A5 — The Hidden Way Below: @Check[type:perception|dc:36], or Venster/Togomor's notes — the Thassilonian shaft beneath the pyramid.
  • A8: two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.ePJcp2ADOjJYYQ5Q]{shining children} (Elite — Trivial · 40 XP @ L15) ward the old stair.
  • A11 — Venster's End: the walled-up card room — first whisper of the ghost upstairs.
  • First floor: gate-stairs, desecrated chapel, the prisons (A19–A22 — the rebellion's missing, freeable: +1 RP), guardrooms (Trivial · 0 XP @ L15 each).

pages.name: A26-A44. Second Floor — the Seneschal's Wing

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A26-A44 — p.356.

Offices, archives, the scriptorium — and the garrison heart: A37/A38 mess and barracks (Trivial · 30 XP @ L15 with the three Yallop matron-sisters). A43: the scriptorium's records (the castle's ledgers = the rebellion's evidence, +1 RP).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor (15)}
A44 — TogomorTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

The bloatmage @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{seneschal} among his instruments — tentacles, cold, quickened missiles, and a familiar with one spell too many. His notes name the Sunken Queen.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A45-A66 — p.362.

  • A45 — Main Entrance Landing: the castle's hardest set-piece if forced (Trivial · 40 XP @ L15 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kayltanyaRedM001]{Kayltanya} + the gate-garrison; CR-18-equivalent — do not knock).
  • A52 — The Throne Room: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto} holds false court (Trivial · 40 XP @ L15 — or a contract negotiation worth more than the fight).
  • A54/A65: gatehouse galleries. A56: the arbiters' last honest chambers.
  • A59 — The Royal Bedroom: a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{False Ileosa} 'discovered' among traps (Trivial · 0 XP @ L16 with her twin — the queen's first decoy lesson).
  • A60 — Sabina's room: spartan; her letters humanize her surrender.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A67-A95 — p.370.

This huge, lofty chamber is illuminated by braziers at the four corners. A soft light also filters from two very high oval windows on the southwestern wall. The twin windows are fitted with panes of blue crystal that filter in some light from outside. Below these crystal 'eyes,' a band of mosaics on the south wall forms a huge map of an ancient, unknown land. The map features a river valley with many villages, each one labeled in a strange language. To the northeast, a shaft drops away in the floor.

Yet the most unusual feature of the room floats and undulates at its center-an amorphous blob of blood, over thirty feet wide, hovers and ripples in the air. Shapes periodically form on its turbulent surface: faces, hands, buildings, and figures that last only long enough to melt back into the horrific mass. Dozens of thin crystal tubes extend from the upper walls of the room to a point just above the shifting mass of blood.

  • A72 — The Grand Salon: a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{False-Ileosa} stage (Trivial · 0 XP @ L16 — three at once, performing grief).
  • A88–A89 — VENSTER'S GHOST: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vensterArabast01]{the kindest reveal} — listen, learn everything, promise him rest.
  • A90 — Domina's study: the castle's oldest secrets. A91–A92: the Epochal Tower clockworks (a climbing route in).
  • A94 — Seawatch: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras} (Low · 60 XP @ L15 — or @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:38] and an empty-treasury ledger sends him home).
  • AFTERSHOCKS: the castle taken (+5 RP), the rebellion surfaces — and Venster's truth points south, to the swamp.

pages.name: Part 3: Legacy of Blood — the Sunken Queen

text.content:
The Sunken Queen — a drowned Thassilonian pyramid in the Mushfens, Sorshen's face carved a hundred feet tall in its flank. Inside: the harvest of Korvosa's blood, the Everdawn Pool, and the queen at her ablutions. The timer: ~10 days of ritual remain (each day below burns a charge toward her permanent apotheosis — give the party two attempts at her).

The approach (B1–B14): the flooded entrance hall (B1 — Trivial · 30 XP @ L16, the matriarch Beirawash: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Lvdykf6wfqzZBlZd]{Black Dragon, Ancient} at Weak — Zarmangarof's dam, owed a grudge for her stolen son); the surface door (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.boggardChamp0001]{boggard champion}, B7) and quarters (5 more, B8 — Trivial · 0 XP @ L16); the crystal tubes (B12) siphoning blood up the pyramid's veins.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier (17)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Fury (12)}
B15-B16 — the Great Hall & the FuriesModerate · 100 XP @ L16

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier} holding court over the wardrobe of queens, with the four @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Furies} (Decabbara, Eveanie, Suishani, Verasia) mustering — thin them here or meet them all at the Pool.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.immortalIchor001]{Immortal Ichor (17)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.soultrapGem00001]{Soultrapping Gem (9)}
B19 — the Blood RepositoryLow · 60 XP @ L16

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.immortalIchor001]{Immortal Ichor} over the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.soultrapGem00001]{Soultrapping Gem} — Ileosa's getaway destination and her reserve of youth. Destroy it before the throne fight and she has nowhere to run and five fewer charges to spend.

pages.name: B20: THE EVERDAWN POOL — the Finale

text.content:
This huge, lofty chamber is illuminated by braziers at the four corners. A soft light also filters from two very high oval windows on the southwestern wall. The twin windows are fitted with panes of blue crystal that filter in some light from outside. Below these crystal 'eyes,' a band of mosaics on the south wall forms a huge map of an ancient, unknown land. The map features a river valley with many villages, each one labeled in a strange language. To the northeast, a shaft drops away in the floor.

Yet the most unusual feature of the room floats and undulates at its center-an amorphous blob of blood, over thirty feet wide, hovers and ripples in the air. Shapes periodically form on its turbulent surface: faces, hands, buildings, and figures that last only long enough to melt back into the horrific mass. Dozens of thin crystal tubes extend from the upper walls of the room to a point just above the shifting mass of blood.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queenIleosa00001]{ILEOSA (21)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.taniniverKaz0001]{Taniniver (17)}
  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{False Ileosa (9)}
THE LAST BATTLEExtreme · 220 XP @ L16

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queenIleosa00001]{QUEEN ILEOSA} (L21) + six @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{false Ileosas} + the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.taniniverKaz0001]{taniniver} (replaceable at 5 charges each) + surviving @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Furies} and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier}. An Extreme-plus battle by design: Serithtial suppresses her regeneration, drains her with every cut, and alone can sunder the Crown. Stealth to the Pool's heart buys a surprise round. She getaways to B19 at ~60 HP, heals, returns — then fights to the death. Killing her destabilizes the Pool: run the Rise of the Dragon immediately.

THE RISE OF THE DRAGON: the Pool's stored blood erupts — Kazavon tries to be born (a CR-25 cataclysm, not a fight): the pyramid shakes apart as a half-formed dragon of blood claws free. The PCs' job is to break the birth — sunder the Crown if they haven't (@Check[type:athletics|dc:40] to hold position in the blood-storm; Serithtial's bearer strikes the Crown), collapse the crystal tubes, and run. Succeed: Kazavon's seed dies forever. Fail to act: see What If Ileosa Wins.

WHAT IF ILEOSA WINS (driven off twice, or the ritual completes): the queen ascends — eternal, beautiful, mad — and Korvosa becomes the seat of a dragon-souled god-queen. The campaign continues as resistance: Kazavon manifests fully within the year; the epilogue arc (Continuing the Campaign) inherits a darker world. The Epidemic Clock reads 10 forever.

pages.name: Chapter Conclusion — Long Live the Queen

text.content:
Immediate repercussions: with Ileosa dead and the Crown sundered, the contract hunts a new holder (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto} — or his master Lorthact — collects unless out-lawyered); the Pool drains; the trapped souls fly home; Korvosa wakes.

The accounting: the rebellion's RP decides the city's dawn (Cressida as Field Marshal-Regent; the seneschal question — Neolandus restored; the succession council). Epidemic Clock: with the harvest dead, Blood Veil's re-seeding ends — the clock empties over the denouement (the CHG-0011b payoff). Sabina's trial and pardon; Trinia's homecoming; Vencarlo passes the Blackjack mantle; Zellara's deck falls finally, gratefully silent. The Shoanti send riders — not for war.

Campaign complete. Epilogue and the optional post-finale arc: see the Appendices & Beyond journal (Continuing the Campaign — Kazavon Rises · Sorshen's Legacy · Rulers of Korvosa).
